articles.title: India bans sugar exports until Sept 2026 to cool local prices
articles.description: India has banned sugar exports until September 30, 2026, in an effort to control local prices. This decision could boost global sugar prices, benefiting competitors like Brazil and Thailand.

articles.title: Modi, others reduce convoy size as govt amplifies pitch for saving fuel
Oil edges up ahead of Trump-Xi meeting in Beijing
articles.description: Oil prices saw a slight increase as investors awaited the meeting between U.S. President Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping, with the Iran war a key focus. Despite U.S. sanctions, China remains a major buyer of Iranian oil, complicating efforts to resolve the conflict.

articles.title: Tech entrepreneur flees Washington due to companies being 'villainized'
articles.description: Washington tech mogul Jesse Proudman joined the exodus to Austin, blasting the state's new "millionaire tax" as an unconstitutional job-killer.

articles.title: Harish Rana’s death symbolises dignity even at the end of life: Supreme Court
articles.description: The Supreme Court on Wednesday observed that the death of Harish Rana, who became the first person in India to be allowed passive euthanasia after spending more than 13 years in a permanent vegetative state, symbolised dignity not only in life but also in death, as it received a report from AIIMS detailing his final days in palliative care.

articles.title: NEET-UG hybrid exam model under study after repeated paper leak rows
Denise Powell wins Democratic primary for Nebraska's 2nd congressional district
articles.description: Political organizer Denise Powell has defeated State Sen. John Cavanaugh to win the Democratic primary in the closely watched race for Nebraska's second congressional district.

articles.title: Jeff Probst's defiant message to 'Survivor 50' critics over Zac Brown backlash: 'I've also got a backbone'
articles.description: Jeff Probst defends "Survivor 50" against superfan criticism, and said celebrity twists like Zac Brown's visit won't be undone by vocal online backlash.

articles.title: Assam Cabinet approves UCC implementation, to be tabled in Assembly on May 26
articles.description: Sarma said that a legislation in this regard would be tabled in the assembly on May 26, the last day of the first session of the new assembly.

articles.title: Pharma, NBFCs see fresh mutual fund buying in April
articles.description: Indian fund managers focused on defensive stocks in April. Pharmaceuticals saw increased investment due to opportunities like the semaglutide patent expiry. NBFCs and asset management companies also received attention. PNB Housing Finance was added as funds shifted towards retail lending. This strategy reflects concerns over oil prices and West Asian geopolitical tensions.

articles.title: Vijay’s TVK wins trust vote with allies, AIADMK rebels’ support
articles.description: Tamil Nadu CM C Joseph Vijay's TVK won a trust vote with 144 votes, amid allegations of horse-trading from opposition parties.

articles.title: Lavrov meets EAM ahead of Brics meet, discusses India-Russia ties

articles.title: Act against mobile apps spreading obscene content, Delhi High Court tells Google and Apple
articles.description: The Delhi High Court on Wednesday asked Google and Apple to act  against mobile applications available on the Google Play Store and  App Store involved in the dissemination of obscene and pornographic content, observing that it cannot permit an entire generation of the country to be ruined.

articles.title: Sebi plans broader use for intraday borrowing by MFs
articles.description: The Securities and Exchange Board of India, Sebi, is proposing changes to how mutual funds use intraday borrowing. Currently limited to redemption payouts, Sebi wants to allow these borrowings as a cash management tool. This aims to improve fund management flexibility and returns by addressing timing mismatches in fund outflows and receivables.

articles.title: DMK boycotts floor test; urges CM to continue with Dravidian model welfare schemes

articles.title: Margin revival, Semaglutide launch to drive Dr Reddy’s growth momentum in FY27
articles.description: Dr Reddy's Laboratories anticipates a gradual recovery this fiscal year. The company faced a profit drop last year due to US business pressures. However, operations in India, Europe, and emerging markets show healthy growth. New launches like Semaglutide and Abatacept are expected to drive future expansion. Margins are set to improve with better product mix and cost controls.

articles.title: Remains of 2nd U.S. soldier who went missing in Morocco have been recovered
articles.description: Spc. Mariyah Symone Collington, 19, of Taveres, Florida, is the second U.S. soldier who fell off a cliff during a recreational hike in Morocco. The remains of 1st Lt. Kendrick Lamont Key Jr. were recovered last week.

articles.title: SC: Tracking devices, alarm buttons must in public vehicles

articles.title: Trading volumes in unlisted shares plunge up to 70%
articles.description: Investor interest in unlisted shares is decreasing. Trading volumes have fallen significantly. This slowdown is due to lower prices and fewer new share offerings. Some popular companies still see activity. However, demand has softened in sectors like defence and hospitality. A stronger stock market is expected to boost this segment again.

articles.title: RBI expected to hold rates in June despite rising inflation, H2 rate hike bets emerge
articles.description: Three out of twelve economists anticipate the Reserve Bank of India will raise policy rates this fiscal year due to inflation concerns, while others predict a prolonged pause. Most economists expect inflation to exceed RBI's forecast for FY27, with the bond market already pricing in tighter monetary policy.

articles.title: What led to the so-called "learning recession" in American schools?

articles.title: How Taiwan factors into Trump's meeting with Xi Jinping
articles.description: President Trump is in Beijing for a high-stakes meeting with his Chinese counterpart, Xi Jinping. He last visited Beijing nearly a decade ago in 2017. This time around, the two are expected to discuss trade, the Middle East and Taiwan. Jonathan Czin, a former senior China analyst at the CIA, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

articles.title: Remains of second US soldier who went missing during military exercises in Morocco have been recovered
articles.description:
Mariyah Symone Collington and Kendrick Lamont Key Jr, who also died, had fallen off a cliff during an off-duty hike
The remains of the second US army soldier who went missing during military exercises in Morocco have been recovered, the army said on Wednesday, ending a multinational search operation that deployed air, naval and artificial intelligence assets.
The soldier was identified as Spc Mariyah Symone Collington of Taveres, Florida, the US Army Europe and Africa said in a statement. She was 19 years old.
 Continue reading...

articles.title: Microsoft’s Edge Copilot update uses AI to pull information from across your tabs
articles.description:
Microsoft Edge is adding a new feature that will allow its Copilot AI chatbot to gather information from all of your open tabs. When you start a conversation with Copilot, you can ask the chatbot questions about what's in your tabs, compare the products you're looking at, summarize your open articles, and more.
In its announcement, Microsoft says you can "select which experiences you want or leave off the ones you don't." The company is retiring Copilot Mode as well, which could similarly draw information from your tabs but offered some agentic features, like the ability to book a reservation on your behalf. Microsoft has since folded these …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Trump administration defends right to ban content moderation experts from US
articles.description:
The Trump administration is fighting for the right to keep some social media moderation advocates out of the US. 
On Wednesday, US District Court Judge James Boasberg heard arguments in a lawsuit between the nonprofit Coalition for Independent Technology Research (CITR) and Secretary of State Marco Rubio and other Trump administration officials. The suit concerns a policy that allows restricting visas to foreign officials who "demand that American tech platforms adopt global content moderation policies." The CITR is arguing for a preliminary injunction blocking the policy, which the State Department has already referenced when it sanctioned …

articles.title: Cartel corruption claims push US-Mexico relations to breaking point
articles.description:
With Mexico under pressure from Trump to tackle drug trafficking groups, analysts say ‘it’s the most tense situation since the 1980s’
Relations between Mexico and the United States are being pushed to breaking point amid accusations by Washington that Mexican officials have been “in bed for years” with drug traffickers, and reports of CIA agents freely operating south of the border.
“There are many who are betting on the defeat and failure of the Mexican government,” said Claudia Sheinbaum tersely on Wednesday, when asked about the allegations at a news conference. ”We want a good relationship with the United States government. What are our limits? The defence of sovereignty and respect for the Mexican people and their dignity.”
 Continue reading...

articles.title: YouTube is courting creators — and sponsors — with streaming shows
articles.description:
In the ongoing fight for content and talent, YouTube is pitching itself as the connector between the creators and advertisers - and marketing its creators not just as the future of social media, but also of advertising, TV, streaming, and entertainment more broadly.
At the company's annual advertiser event in New York on Wednesday, YouTube introduced a new slate of exclusive shows coming to the platform, hosted by some big names: a travel show with Trevor Noah, a Met Gala documentary series from podcaster Alex Cooper, a new series from Kareem Rahma, the host of the popular show Subway Takes, and more. The pitch to advertisers: Invest in the …

articles.title: AMD’s best CPU tech for gamers is coming to workstations too
articles.description:
For the first time, AMD is including its 3D V-Cache tech in its commercial workstation processors with a refreshed line of Ryzen PRO 9000 series chips. Up until now, AMD's 3D V-Cache chips have mainly been geared toward gamers, but they've also gotten better at creative tasks over recent years. In our review of the Ryzen 9 9950X3D last year, Tom Warren crowned it "the best CPU for both gaming and creator tasks," noting that it "greatly improves creator workloads" compared to the Ryzen 7 9800X3D. 
According to AMD, the 3D V-Cache tech will be useful for "complex, data-intensive workloads such as simulation, rendering and real-time visualizat …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Fighting Trump will make or break Disney’s new CEO
articles.description:
A week ago, newly appointed Disney CEO Josh D'Amaro was busy regaling investors with plans to turn Disney Plus into the company's "digital centerpiece." By last Friday, though, his attention had presumably shifted to a fight with the Trump administration over free speech.
Disney-owned ABC has now accused the administration of violating its First Amendment rights with an ongoing investigation into The View. D'Amaro - the former head of Disney's parks division - might have wanted his legacy to be defined by corporate synergy and a souped-up version of Disney Plus. But this fight with Donald Trump and the Federal Communications Commission is l …

articles.title: India Bans Sugar Exports Until End-September to Protect Supply
articles.description: India, the world’s second-largest producer of sugar, has banned exports until the end of September, according to an official notice, as the government seeks to protect local supplies.

articles.title: Modi, others reduce convoy size as govt amplifies pitch for saving fuel
Oil edges up ahead of Trump-Xi meeting in Beijing
articles.description: Oil prices saw a slight increase as investors awaited the meeting between U.S. President Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping, with the Iran war a key focus. Despite U.S. sanctions, China remains a major buyer of Iranian oil, complicating efforts to resolve the conflict.

articles.title: Tech entrepreneur flees Washington due to companies being 'villainized'
articles.description: Washington tech mogul Jesse Proudman joined the exodus to Austin, blasting the state's new "millionaire tax" as an unconstitutional job-killer.

articles.title: Harish Rana’s death symbolises dignity even at the end of life: Supreme Court
articles.description: The Supreme Court on Wednesday observed that the death of Harish Rana, who became the first person in India to be allowed passive euthanasia after spending more than 13 years in a permanent vegetative state, symbolised dignity not only in life but also in death, as it received a report from AIIMS detailing his final days in palliative care.

articles.title: NEET-UG hybrid exam model under study after repeated paper leak rows
Denise Powell wins Democratic primary for Nebraska's 2nd congressional district
articles.description: Political organizer Denise Powell has defeated State Sen. John Cavanaugh to win the Democratic primary in the closely watched race for Nebraska's second congressional district.

articles.title: Denise Powell projected to win Democratic primary in key Nebraska district
articles.description: The state's electoral system was a key issue in the 2nd Congressional District primary to replace GOP Rep. Don Bacon.

articles.title: Jeff Probst's defiant message to 'Survivor 50' critics over Zac Brown backlash: 'I've also got a backbone'
articles.description: Jeff Probst defends "Survivor 50" against superfan criticism, and said celebrity twists like Zac Brown's visit won't be undone by vocal online backlash.

articles.title: India bans sugar exports until Sept 2026 to cool local prices
articles.description: India has banned sugar exports until September 30, 2026, in an effort to control local prices. This decision could boost global sugar prices, benefiting competitors like Brazil and Thailand.

articles.title: Lavrov meets EAM ahead of Brics meet, discusses India-Russia ties
S Jaishankar, Russia's Lavrov Hold Talks Ahead Of BRICS Meet
articles.description: Jaishankar met Lavrov, shortly after the Russian foreign minister landed in New Delhi to attend a two-day conclave of the BRICS member states beginning Thursday.

topStories.title: Remains of 2nd U.S. soldier who went missing in Morocco have been recovered
topStories.description: Spc. Mariyah Symone Collington, 19, of Taveres, Florida, is the second U.S. soldier who fell off a cliff during a recreational hike in Morocco. The remains of 1st Lt. Kendrick Lamont Key Jr. were recovered last week.

topStories.title: What we know about hantavirus cases tied to deadly cruise ship outbreak
topStories.description: Health officials have identified at least 11 confirmed or suspected cases of hantavirus tied to an outbreak on the M/V Hondius cruise ship.

topStories.description: President Trump's trip to China gets underway; DHS Secretary Markwayne Mullin details what ICE agents will be doing at World Cup games.

topStories.title: Musk flew to China near end of OpenAI trial, after judge warned he could be recalled
topStories.description: Elon Musk arrived in Beijing on Wednesday, as his $150 billion lawsuit against OpenAI's Sam Altman played out. But a judge told Musk last month he may be recalled to a California courtroom for further testimony at the request of OpenAI lawyers.

topStories.description: A clade I mpox case is confirmed in Connecticut as global health officials also monitor newly reported hantavirus cases in Spain and France.

topStories.description: Donald Trump China Visit LIVE: The US President is on a two-day visit to China during which, he will hold a Summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping to talk trade, Iran war, disruptions caused by it and arms sale to Taiwan.

dailyNews.title: How Taiwan factors into Trump's meeting with Xi Jinping
dailyNews.description: President Trump is in Beijing for a high-stakes meeting with his Chinese counterpart, Xi Jinping. He last visited Beijing nearly a decade ago in 2017. This time around, the two are expected to discuss trade, the Middle East and Taiwan. Jonathan Czin, a former senior China analyst at the CIA, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

dailyNews.title: Margaret Cho blames Trump and ICE for missing out on hit HBO Max series
dailyNews.description: Actress Margaret Cho claimed she lost a role in the HBO Max series "Heated Rivalry" because she was afraid of ICE detention under the Trump administration.

dailyNews.description: Trump and Xi's summit comes at a crucial moment for the global economy, as the war on Iran continues to disrupt trade.

dailyNews.title: Which teams have the most valuable college football rosters heading into 2026 season?
dailyNews.description: SEC and Big Ten dominate NIL spending estimates with 12 of the top 15 most valuable rosters, with defending champs Indiana not on the list ahead of 2026 college football season.

dailyNews.title: Morgan Wallen fan goes viral kicking a phone out of someone's hand as she's being escorted out in handcuffs
dailyNews.description: A woman at a Morgan Wallen concert went viral after perfectly kicking a fan's phone while walking down stairs in handcuffs with a police officer.

dailyNews.title: US renews offer of $100 million in aid to Cuba if it cooperates
dailyNews.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio, speaking last week in Rome, said that Cuba had rejected an offer of $100 million in assistance, an assertion denied by the communist government in Havana.

dailyNews.description: Asia markets eyed gains ahead of closely watched Trump-Xi summit on trade, tariffs and technology restrictions.

dailyNews.description: Can Japan be a major player in the international defense sector, like its neighbor South Korea?

dailyNews.description: In the most divisive vote ever for a Fed chair, Warsh, 56, won confirmation to take over for Jerome Powell.

dailyNews.title: Trump administration defends right to ban content moderation experts from US
dailyNews.description:
The Trump administration is fighting for the right to keep some social media moderation advocates out of the US. 
On Wednesday, US District Court Judge James Boasberg heard arguments in a lawsuit between the nonprofit Coalition for Independent Technology Research (CITR) and Secretary of State Marco Rubio and other Trump administration officials. The suit concerns a policy that allows restricting visas to foreign officials who "demand that American tech platforms adopt global content moderation policies." The CITR is arguing for a preliminary injunction blocking the policy, which the State Department has already referenced when it sanctioned …
Read the full story at The Verge.

dailyNews.title: Trump taps David Venturella, former private prison executive, to lead ICE
dailyNews.description: Venturella will helm the immigration enforcement agency after previously serving in an executive role at GEO Group.

dailyNews.title: Cartel corruption claims push US-Mexico relations to breaking point
dailyNews.description:
With Mexico under pressure from Trump to tackle drug trafficking groups, analysts say ‘it’s the most tense situation since the 1980s’
Relations between Mexico and the United States are being pushed to breaking point amid accusations by Washington that Mexican officials have been “in bed for years” with drug traffickers, and reports of CIA agents freely operating south of the border.
“There are many who are betting on the defeat and failure of the Mexican government,” said Claudia Sheinbaum tersely on Wednesday, when asked about the allegations at a news conference. ”We want a good relationship with the United States government. What are our limits? The defence of sovereignty and respect for the Mexican people and their dignity.”
 Continue reading...

dailyNews.title: Foreign ticket holders from World Cup teams' countries won't have to pay bonds to enter U.S.
dailyNews.description: The Trump administration is suspending a requirement that visitors from countries that have qualified for the World Cup and bought tickets for the tournament pay as much as $15,000 in bonds to enter the U.S.

dailyNews.title: White House to host 9-hour prayer festival focused on Christian roots of U.S.
dailyNews.description: Pete Hegseth, Marco Rubio and Mike Johnson will speak at the event, which centers on the idea that the founders wanted the U.S. to be explicitly Christian.

dailyNews.title: Senate Confirms Kevin Warsh as Fed Chair
dailyNews.description: President Trump’s pick to replace Jerome H. Powell takes the helm at a highly uncertain moment for the economy and with heightened concern about the Federal Reserve’s ability to operate independently.

dailyNews.title: Fighting Trump will make or break Disney’s new CEO
dailyNews.description:
A week ago, newly appointed Disney CEO Josh D'Amaro was busy regaling investors with plans to turn Disney Plus into the company's "digital centerpiece." By last Friday, though, his attention had presumably shifted to a fight with the Trump administration over free speech.
Disney-owned ABC has now accused the administration of violating its First Amendment rights with an ongoing investigation into The View. D'Amaro - the former head of Disney's parks division - might have wanted his legacy to be defined by corporate synergy and a souped-up version of Disney Plus. But this fight with Donald Trump and the Federal Communications Commission is l …
Read the full story at The Verge.

dailyNews.title: Trade, Iran and Taiwan on the agenda as Trump arrives in China for high-stakes talks with Xi
dailyNews.description: The US president says his first request to Xi would be to  "open up" China, while Beijing is expected to press Trump on the status of Taiwan.

dailyNews.title: What to know as Trump visits Xi in China
dailyNews.description: Trump has landed in Beijing for the first visit by a US leader since his own in 2017.

dailyNews.title: Facing headwinds at home, Trump signals eagerness to make deals at China summit
dailyNews.description: Under pressure from Beijing, the president has indicated an openness to rethinking U.S. support for Taiwan, alarming its backers.
